matches.match_rationale: Restorative practice requires structured dialogue scripts, incident reflection prompts, victim-offender conference facilitation guides, and community service agreement templates — all natural language artifacts that staff currently lack. AI can generate context-specific restorative documentation (e.g., a tailored conference agenda given the nature of the incident) far faster than staff can draft them manually, lowering the barrier to using restorative tools at all.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-assisted case tool that generates a customized restorative intervention plan — including facilitation script, reflection questions for the student, and a letter to affected parties — based on the incident type and student history entered by the staff member.

matches.match_rationale: Incident reports and discipline logs contain free-text descriptions that can be classified by root-cause category (peer conflict, trauma response, unmet academic need, boundary-testing) — the exact causal factors the pain point identifies as unaddressed. Classifying incidents by underlying cause is a prerequisite for routing to the right intervention tier rather than defaulting to punishment.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An incident triage classifier that reads a staff-submitted incident description and outputs a root-cause category and recommended intervention tier (warning → restorative conference → exclusion), providing a structured decision aid rather than leaving it to individual staff discretion.

matches.match_rationale: Students who repeat offenses after punitive-only responses are identifiable in retrospect from attendance, prior incident frequency, academic disengagement signals, and welfare referrals — structured data most multi-site operators already hold. A propensity model predicting re-offense or dropout risk post-discipline event gives welfare coordinators a data-driven basis to escalate to restorative or pastoral intervention before the next incident.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A post-incident re-offense propensity score surfaced in the student management system 48 hours after a discipline event, flagging high-risk students for a proactive welfare check or restorative referral before the next incident cycle begins.

matches.match_rationale: Staff lack restorative training, which is the stated causal factor. A conversational agent can guide a staff member through a restorative conversation in real time — prompting the right questions, offering alternative phrasings when a student becomes defensive, and keeping the interaction on track — effectively substituting for formal restorative training in the moment.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A staff-facing chat assistant embedded in the school's pastoral care platform that walks a teacher or welfare officer through a structured restorative conversation with a student, providing live coaching prompts and capturing the agreed-upon repair plan as a structured record.
